basic_details.job_title: IPR Apprentices Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 50 Posts
basic_details.job_type: Apprenticeship training (one year) under Apprentices (Amendment) Act, 1973
basic_details.job_location: Institute for Plasma Research (as per institute requirements)
basic_details.short_information: Institute for Plasma Research (IPR), an aided institute under the Department of Atomic Energy, Government of India, has released Advt. No. 05/2026 for engagement of Graduate and Technician Apprentices under the Apprentices (Amendment) Act, 1973. Eligible Degree and Diploma holders in Engineering and General Stream can apply online through the NATS portal for one-year apprenticeship training as per Government of India guidelines.

application_fee.details: The advertisement does not mention any application fee; applications are invited online through the NATS portal as per Apprenticeship rules.

age_limit.details: The notification specifies academic year conditions for Degree/Diploma passing but does not explicitly give an age limit; eligibility is tied to year of passing. | Key points: | Candidates should have passed Degree/Diploma in the respective branch/discipline in 2024, 2025 or 2026. Candidates who have passed Degree/Diploma in 2023 or earlier are not eligible.

qualification.details: Basic Eligibility | Candidates must be Degree or Diploma holders in the relevant Engineering branches or Graduates in General Stream as specified. Candidates must have passed in 2024, 2025, or 2026 only; those who passed in 2023 or before are not eligible. Candidates who have already undergone apprenticeship training elsewhere will not be considered.

post_wise_qualification.Required Qualification: B.Tech or B.E in Computer Engineering
B.Tech or B.E in Civil Engineering
B.Tech or B.E in Electrical Engineering
B.Tech or B.E in Electronics & Communication Engineering
B.Tech or B.E in Mechanical Engineering
B.Tech or B.E in Instrumentation & Control Engineering
Graduate in Commerce / Arts / Business Administration

selection_process: Selection will be purely on merit based on percentage of marks in the respective Degree/Diploma in Engineering/Non-Engineering. Wherever CGPA/SGPA/letter grade is awarded

candidates must indicate equivalent percentage in the online form as per Institute/University norms and produce conversion formula at the time of document verification. The list of selected candidates will be displayed on the IPR website only. Candidature will be provisional and subject to verification of certificates

testimonials and medical fitness. Any form of canvassing will lead to cancellation of candidature. Incorrect/incomplete applications will be summarily rejected and no correspondence will be entertained.
how_to_apply: Step 1: Register on the Board of Apprenticeship Training (BOAT) NATS portal at https://nats.education.gov.in and obtain a valid registration number.
Step 2: Candidates meeting the prescribed eligibility criteria should submit their online application for IPR apprenticeship through the NATS portal (https://nats.education.gov.in).
Step 3: Ensure that Degree/Diploma details, percentage (or converted percentage from CGPA/SGPA) and branch are correctly filled as per institute norms.
Step 4: Regularly visit the IPR website for latest updates regarding apprenticeship notification, selection lists and further instructions.

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies in IPR Apprentices Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 50 apprenticeship seats have been notified — 32 Graduate Apprentices and 18 Technician (Diploma) Apprentices — under Institute for Plasma Research (IPR) Advt. No. 05/2026.

faq.question: Q2. What types of apprentice posts are offered in IPR Apprentices Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: IPR is offering one‑year apprenticeship training for Graduate Apprentices (Degree holders) and Technician Apprentices (Diploma holders) in Engineering branches and General Stream.

faq.question: Q3. What are the important dates for IPR Apprentices Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: The last date for submission of online applications through the NATS portal is 11 May 2026; candidates must complete registration and application before this deadline.

faq.question: Q4. Is there any application fee for IPR Apprentices 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: The official advertisement does not mention any application fee; applications are invited free of cost through the NATS portal as per Apprenticeship rules.

faq.question: Q5. Which branches are eligible for Graduate (Degree) Apprentices at IPR?
faq.answer: Ans: Graduate Apprentices are invited in Computer Engineering, Civil, Electrical, Electronics & Communication, Mechanical, Instrumentation & Control, and a General Stream for B.Com, B.A, and B.B.A graduates.

faq.question: Q6. Which branches are eligible for Technician (Diploma) Apprentices at IPR?
faq.answer: Ans: Technician Apprentices are invited in Computer Engineering, Civil, Electrical, Electronics & Communication, Mechanical, and Instrumentation & Control disciplines.

faq.question: Q7. What is the educational qualification and passing year criteria for IPR Apprentices Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: Candidates must hold a relevant Degree or Diploma in the notified branch and must have passed in 2024, 2025, or 2026 only; those who passed in 2023 or earlier, or who have already undergone apprenticeship training, are not eligible.

faq.question: Q8. What is the monthly stipend for IPR Graduate and Technician Apprentices 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: Graduate Apprentices will receive a consolidated stipend of ₹13,500 per month, while Technician (Diploma) Apprentices will receive ₹12,000 per month during the one‑year training.

faq.question: Q9. What is the selection process for IPR Apprentices Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: Selection is purely merit‑based on the percentage of marks in the qualifying Degree/Diploma; where CGPA/SGPA is awarded, candidates must provide equivalent percentage as per their institute’s conversion formula at document verification.

faq.question: Q10. How can candidates apply online for IPR Apprentices Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: Eligible candidates must first register on the BOAT NATS portal (nats.education.gov.in) and then submit their application for IPR Apprenticeship through the same portal, ensuring all academic and percentage details are correctly filled, and regularly check the IPR website for selection lists and updates.
